english: ma bcud če-la bza če-ba (ma bcud che la bza' che ba) nutritive and substantial food Mil. nt. Comp. bza-ytád (bza' gtad), bzá-bai ytád-so (bza' ba'i gtad so) store of provisions, bzá-ytad-méd-pa (bza' gtad med pa) not having such a store Mil. — bza-mí (bza' mi) 1. = Kyo-śug (khyo shug) husband and wife, byéd-pa (byed pa) to become husband and wife, to marry each other, kyod daṅ ṅa yñis bza-mi byao (khyod dang nga gnyis bza' mi bya'o) we will marry each other Glr.; bza-mír byin-gyis rlób-pa (bza' mir byin gyis rlob pa) to give the nuptial benediction, to unite in wedlock, to marry Glr.; dbúl-po bza-mi yñis (dbul po bza' mi gnyis) a poor married couple Glr. 2. in a wider sense: household, bza-mí ñyi-śu-rtsa-yñis (bza' mi nyi shu rtsa gnyis) a household of twenty two persons Mil. — bza-med (bza' med) ill-fed, lean Mil. — bzá-tso (bza' tsho) plur. of bza (bza'). — *za-dá* (*za zla*) (lit. za-zlá (za zla)) W., C. partner, wife. — bza-śiṅ (bza' shing) fruit-tree, bza-śiṅ-rá-ba (bza' shing ra ba) orchard, bza-śiṅ-ra-ba-sruṅ-pa (bza' shing ra ba srung pa) watchman or keeper of it Dzl. — bza-śug (bza' shug) (vulg. *śúb* (*shub*)) = bza-mi (bza' mi) C.
sometimes for zar (zar); bzár-ba (bzar ba) v. °dzár-ba ('dzar ba).
v. zá-ba (za ba) and yzás-pa (gzas pa).

(sometimes yzi (gzi), zi (zi)), drunken fit, intoxication, stupefaction; bzi sáṅs-te (bzi sangs te) having become sober again after intoxication Glr.; *zi-ćaṅ* (*zi chang*) W. intoxicated, muddled, bzi-ba (bzi ba) 1. vb. to become intoxicated, to get drunk, bzi-bar gyúr-ba (bzi bar gyur ba) id.; bzi-bar byéd-pa (bzi bar byed pa) to intoxicate, to make drunk Cs. 2. sbst. state of intoxication. 3. adj. drunk, intoxicated C.

v. °dzín-pa ('dzin pa); it is used as an adv. in the form of bzúṅs-te (bzungs te), e.g. dei núb-mo-nas bzúṅs-te (de'i nub mo nas bzungs te) from that evening (prop. beginning with that evening), ever since that evening Mil.; tses brgyad-nas bzúṅs-te nyai bar-du (tshes brgyad nas bzungs te nya'i bar du) during the time from the 8th. to the 15th. (day of the month).
also bze (bze) Sch.: pain, bze-re-ćan (bze re can) suffering pain, bze-ré byéd-pa (bze re byed pa) to inflict pain, to torment. (Cs.: 'indignation; angry; to be angry with.')

1. in comp.: pyag-bzéd (phyag bzed) (hand-)basin Cs.; lhuṅ-bzéd (lhung bzed) beggar's bowl, alms-pot, frq; bzed-snód (bzed snod) salver Sch; bzed-żál (bzed zhal) Lex., lso żal-bzéd (zhal bzed) Cs.: 'spitting-box; acc. to oral expl. a cup into which the higher class of people skim off the superabundant grease swimming on the tea (v. ja (ja)); bzéd-pa (bzed pa) v. °dzéd-pa ('dzed pa). — 2. bzed-snyóms-pa (bzed snyoms pa) wire-drawing Sch.

1. work, labour, bzoi rnam-gyur (bzo'i rnam gyur) the beauty of a work or workmanship Glr.; bzo rgya-nág-gi lugs (bzo rgya nag gi lugs) as to the workmanship it is in Chinese style Glr. (by some the word is taken in these passages in the signification 3). — bzó-la sréd-pa (bzo la sred pa) liking labour, laborious, = las (las) Stg.; *zo te-rél, mi-la ma (s)tan* (*zo te rel, mi la ma bstan*) W. the work is not yet finished, do not let people see it yet! snai bzo byed-gin °dug-pas (sna'i bzo byed gin 'dug pas) being just occupied with working out the noses Glr. — 2. manufacture, art, trade, handicraft, rin-po-čei (rin po che'i) art of a jeweler, gos- (gos-) trade of a tailor, dṅul- (dngul-) art of a silversmith, lčags- (lcags-) trade of a blacksmith, tag- (thag-) of a rope-maker, rdo- (rdo-) of a stone-cutter, rtsig- (rtsig-) of a mason, bzaṅ- (bzang-) of a copper-smith, śiṅ- (shing-) of a joiner or carpenter, gser- (gser-) art of a goldsmith, lha- (lha-) of an image-maker, lham-bzo (lham bzo) trade of a shoemaker. — 3. also zo (zo), figure, image, picture, resemblance, = dbyibs (dbyibs), *ă-me zo dug* (*a me zo dug*) W. he is the exact likeness of his mother; appearance, physical constitution, v. zo (zo). — 4. sometimes for bzó-pa (bzo pa), bzó-bo (bzo bo), so that all the words enumerated sub 2 may also denote the artist or workman. — Comp. and deriv. bzó-kaṅ (bzo khang) workshop. — bzo-kyád (bzo khyad), bzoi kyad (bzo'i khyad) Glr. work of art, masterpiece, elegant piece of workmanship. — bzo-kyúd (bzo khyud), bzo-kyun (bzo khyun) Cs.: 1. potter's wheel. 2. a hydraulic machine(?). — bzo-grá (bzo grwa) academy of arts, mechanics' institution Cs. — bzo-rgyú (bzo rgyu) working-materials Glr. — *zó-bsta(?), zób-sta, zó-sta* (*zo bsta, zob sta, zo sta*) W. form, fashion, e.g. style of a house, its architecture; form, of a bottle, a lamp or candle stick, of any production of art; *zor-dó* (*zor do*) anvil-stone W. (bzo-rdo (bzo rdo)). — bzó-pa (bzo pa) artist, mechanic, dṅul-bzo-pa (dngul bzo pa), silversmith, and so forth. — bzo-dpón (bzo dpon) master, over journey-men or the students of an art. — bzó-ba (bzo ba), pf. bzos (bzos), to make, to manufacture C. (for the byéd-pa (byed pa) of B., and *čo-če* (*co ce*) of W.), *par zó-wa* (*par bzo ba*) to print; *sém-kyi zó-wa* (*sems kyi bzo ba*) C. to frame in one's mind, contrive, invent; *zo- (*zo-)
